assertions.text: Picks regress (not TAP / isolation / module) as the flavor.
Names the three required files: sql/<name>.sql, expected/<name>.out, parallel_schedule.
Tells the user to GENERATE expected/.out by running and copying from results/, not type by hand.
Mentions the max-20-per-group rule in parallel_schedule.
Warns about volatile output (OIDs, timestamps, row order) before committing.
Provides a single-test fast-loop command (pg_regress or meson).
Points to regression.diffs as first stop on failure.
Picks TAP and places it under source/src/test/recovery/t/NNN_name.pl.
Mentions appending NNN (don't renumber existing tests) for backport safety.
Mentions wiring into recovery/meson.build.
Provides a working skeleton with PostgreSQL::Test::Cluster, init(allows_streaming=>1), init_from_backup, start.
Mentions wait_for_replay_catchup (or equivalent sync) as the way to wait for the standby.
Gives the one-line single-TAP-test runner (meson test --suite recovery NNN_name or PROVE_TESTS=).
Points to build/testrun/<suite>/<test>/log/regress_log_* as first place to look on failure.
Honestly notes that there is no direct 'assert WAL record of type X applied' API — verify via observable state/LSN.
Picks isolation spec, not TAP, for two-session heavyweight-lock contention.
Names the three files: specs/<name>.spec, expected/<name>.out, isolation_schedule.
Calls out that explicit permutation lines are MANDATORY when steps block (else auto-permutations hang).
Mentions isolationtester only detects heavyweight locks via pg_locks (LWLock/buffer pin invisible → injection points).
Provides at least a sketch of session/step/permutation grammar.
Discourages _1.out variants; recommends markers (s1(*), s1(s2), s1(s2 notices N)) for stabilization.
Provides the single-spec runner command (pg_isolation_regress) and points to output_iso/regression.diffs on failure.

notes: Both attempts pass nearly every assertion. The SKILL is well-structured: decision tree + wiring table + run/debug commands map cleanly onto the kind of prompt a real PG contributor asks. Attempt B is terser (skipped a full skeleton in eval 2, skipped a full spec sketch in eval 3) but still correct — these are 'partial' because they reduce a beginner's chance of getting it right without re-reading. No assertion failed outright; nothing in the SKILL led either attempt astray.
